attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who launches ambitious long-range moves when GC or victory requires it, capable of matching explosive finale accelerations from pure puncheurs like Van der Poel in sprint situations.
team_tactics: Functions both as protected finisher and tactical weapon; teams employ delayed positioning (hitting front post-final corner) to negate early lead-outs, though conservative wait-for-teammate tactics can be counterproductive to his winning chances.
terrain_preference: Thrives on punchy mixed terrain combining climbs and technical sections (Strade Bianche success); demonstrates exceptional versatility transitioning between hilly classics and stage race finales within same week.
vulnerability: Conservative team tactics limiting his individual winning opportunities when designated to wait for stronger teammates; falls marginally short in pure power sprints against top classics specialists.
key_patterns: Peaks exceptionally early season, maintaining form across consecutive race weeks (2017-2018 evidence).
Shows strong form resilience: podiums in different race types within same week (3 observations).
Capable of matching Monument-level attacks but rarely converting to victories when team tactics intervene.
Excels at calculated long-range GC attacks using time bonifications in stage races (2020 example).
Performs consistently on technical climbs like Monte Sante Marie across multiple years.
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 showed ability to match Van der Poel's best attacks in Dwars door Vlaanderen, indicating maintained high-level classics form despite team role conflicts
